articles.title: Messi received like royalty in New York ahead of World Cup final

children.content: Argentina's Lionel Messi has received rock star reception in New York City ahead of the World Cup final against Spain, but how much of a lasting impact will this tournament make in a crowded sporting market?

image.altText: Lionel Messi winks and gives a thumbs up as he sits on a stage

articles.title: How an Italian sculptor created the World Cup trophy that became an icon

children.content: It has become the most iconic trophy in world sport, and it all started in a small design studio in Milan more than 50 years ago.

image.altText: Two footballers hold a golden trophy.

children.content: France and England are playing for pride and third place in the World Cup, as Kylian Mbappe and Harry  Kane make final bids for the golden boot. Follow live.

items.title: Golden boot and third place up for grabs as France faces England

updates.title: A fair few changes for France
Mass changes for England's starting XI
Welcome to the 'Bronze Final' – France vs England

items.title: A new tech revolution promises to reverse aging. Here's the reality

children.content: The fear of aging is driving a wave of biohacking businesses promising to help restore youth and fight disease. Four Corners investigates the race to reverse the aging process.

image.altText: a man wearing a red light helmet

items.title: Two US service members 'killed in action' in Jordan

children.content: The US and Iran have intensified attacks since an interim ceasefire deal signed a month ago fell apart, raising the possibility of a return to all-out war.

image.altText: Several ships in the ocean, with a mountainous coastline in the background

items.title: Pauline Hanson delivers CPAC London speech to mostly empty seats

children.content: Pauline Hanson has ended her trip to Britain and Europe on a muted note, after about 120 people attended her speech at a political conference in London on Saturday.

image.altText: Pauline Hanson wears a blue business dress on stage with a big screen behind her

items.title: Hanson faces rough political seas beyond the Mediterranean

children.content: Many voters are unhappy with the hand they feel successive governments have dealt them and no one has tapped into that better than Pauline Hanson.

image.altText: Pauline Hanson speaks to reporters among a crowd, wearing a brown and black jacket.

items.title: My dad couldn’t express his love for me. I chose to be a different man

children.content: These men are being the role models they never had. They hope to curb Australia's masculinity crisis.

gallery.altText: a young boy smiles at the camera, while hugging his dad whose face we can't see

items.title: Sara Albrecht took on Trump's tariffs. She got 'violent' calls

children.content: When a law centre decided to fight Donald Trump's self-dubbed "liberation day" tariffs, its chairperson was acutely conscious of possible "retaliation" from a Republican president who takes matters "personally".

image.altText: A woman stands in front of multiple microphones with two men in suits standing behind her.

items.title: As Europe follows Australia's social media ban, the US opts for own way

children.content: More and more countries are following Australia's lead and introducing social media bans for children, but US policies are piecemeal and vary from state to state.

image.altText: Alexis sits at an outdoor table with a metal fence and trees in the background.

items.title: Behind a secret safe, police find 74kgs of gold bars in suitcases

children.content: Indonesian police investigating suspected corruption discover millions of dollars worth of currency and 74 kilograms of gold in a safe hidden in a luxury house near Jakarta.

gallery.altText: Gold bars on two suitcases pictured from above.

thumbnail.altText: Gold bars on two suitcases pictured from above.

items.title: 'Lack of evidence' means Lao officials won't lay charges over teens' deaths

children.content: Investigators in Laos gave one of their first detailed accounts of the investigation into the deaths of six people, including 19-year-old Australians Holly Morton-Bowles and Bianca Jones, saying they don't have enough evidence to lay charges because autopsies were not carried out.

image.altText: Side-by-side photos of Bianca Jones and Holly Bowles, two Melbourne teens who have been hospitalised in Thailand

items.title: Quiet change to Nauru policing powers could thwart legal challenges

children.content: Advocates question the timing of law changes that coincide with mounting federal court challenges from individuals fighting deportation.

image.altText: A Nauru man in a black police officers shirt, stands with his hands on a desk.

items.title: A US tech start-up wants to turn night into day. The question is why?

children.content: Reflect Orbital has won initial approval for a plan to put thousands of mirrors in orbit that would be visible from anywhere on Earth. But will anyone pay for sunlight?

gallery.altText: A large circle of sunlight illuminates a city at night

items.title: Eight killed, 60 wounded, as Ukraine attacks Russian warehouses

children.content: Two sprawling warehouses of Russia's major online retailer are hit by Ukrainian drones, local officials say, with massive plumes of smoke towering over a facility at Elektrostal, 50 kilometres east of Moscow.

image.altText: Two huge plumes of grey smoke are rising from the scene of a warehouse fire.

items.title: How brighter, safer headlights may cause problems for other drivers

children.content: Bright LED headlights improve visibility for motorists and make them significantly safer at night, but what about the driver coming the other way?

image.altText: Glare from a new car's headlights at night.

items.title: Like The Beatles and Beach Boys? You're going to love The Lemon Twigs

children.content: These New York twenty-somethings make music indebted to Brian Wilson, Todd Rundgren and the Fab Four. But do The Lemon Twigs get sick of the comparisons?

items.title: Wallabies thrash Italy as Schmidt era ends in triumph

children.content: The Wallabies record a convincing 57-10 victory over Italy in Perth, as Joe Schmidt's coaching tenure finishes on a winning note.

image.altText: Jeremy Williams and Joe Schmidt share a laugh.

items.title: 'It's not right': Dragons fume after pushing Warriors

children.content: Second-placed New Zealand Warriors squeak to a nervy 20-12 victory over wooden-spoon favourite St George Illawarra, although the Dragons fume over narrow refereeing calls.

image.altText: A group of rugby league players in red and white celebrate a try, jumping on top of eachother

items.title: Exercise Pitch Black 2026 war games take flight

items.title: Cattle farmers plant trees for sustainable aviation fuel

children.content: A north Queensland farming family is trialling planting biofuel-producing trees alongside their cattle.

image.altText: A mother and daughter farmers standing in a field patting cattle.

items.title: Indian activist on hunger strike forcibly removed by police

children.content: Sonam Wangchuk, who began a hunger strike last month in solidarity with India's youth party after an examination scandal affected millions of students, was forced to go to hospital by court order due to his "deteriorating health condition".

image.altText: A man lies on his back against a pillow during a hunger strike while a doctor examines him.

items.title: Massive fire becomes Norway's largest in modern times

children.content: The largest residential fire in modern Norwegian history destroys more than 100 homes near Oslo in what a local official described as "a terrible incident".

gallery.altText: Smoke rises from terraced houses on a hillside. The picture is looking down on the village which is surrounded by trees..

thumbnail.altText: Smoke rises from terraced houses on a hillside. The picture is looking down on the village which is surrounded by trees..

items.title: Early-onset bowel cancer becoming more prevalent as signs are missed

children.content: Georgie Cauchi had blood in her stool, constant pain and was struggling to get out of bed, but still in her 20s doctors told her she was too young to have bowel cancer. When it was finally discovered several years later, the tumour had grown so large it was blocking her entire bowel.

image.altText: A young woman in a white shirt sitting in her living room.

items.title: 'Failed' university scheme hurting home ownership prospects

children.content: There are calls for the government to dump a scheme that has more than doubled the cost of some degrees.

image.altText: man stares off camera with a thoughtful expression. He is outside.

items.title: India successfully launches nation's first private orbital rocket

children.content: India's first privately developed orbital rocket, the Vikram-1, has successfully lifted off on its maiden orbital mission.

image.altText: India's Skyroot takes off

articles.title: Why India is reopening a nuclear power plant built in the 1960s

children.content: Despite repeated setbacks and isolation, India continues to hold onto its nuclear dream.

items.title: Failed interns get a second chance to become doctors in new Aussie drama

children.content: Growing up, Ioane Sa'ula thought he'd probably play rugby league professionally, but an injury scuppered his plans. Now, he's the romantic lead of new Aussie medical drama, The F Ward.

gallery.altText: Ioane Sa'ula, Samoan Australian, mid-20s, in orange scrubs, sits in a hospital waiting area, leaning forward, hands clasped.

thumbnail.altText: Ioane Sa'ula, Samoan Australian, mid-20s, in orange scrubs, sits in a hospital waiting area, leaning forward, hands clasped.

items.title: Meet 'Jimothy', the viral, short-spined raccoon who roams Seattle

children.content: A raccoon with a rare birth defect has amassed a legion of fans after a video of it darting across a footpath and running up a set of stairs racked up more than 6.8 million views in just three days.

image.altText: A raccoon with a severly shortened spin walks across some grass.

items.title: 'Dangerously close to becoming normalised': Government responds to Islamophobia report

children.content: The Albanese government's long-awaited response to its special envoy's report on Islamophobia has rejected calls for an overhaul of counter-terror laws.

image.altText: A man wearing a suit with a coat, a red tie and glasses.

items.title: Olympic swimmers return to iconic Sydney pool for 50-year reunion

children.content: Members of the 1976 Australian Olympic swim team return to North Sydney Olympic pool to celebrate their 50-year reunion and revisit the venue that shaped their careers.

items.title: Researchers working on artificial placentas for premature babies

children.content: The aim of the technology is to give babies born too soon a few more weeks to grow and develop in womb-like conditions, and a better chance of a long and healthy life.

image.altText: The foot and hand of a premmie baby with hand holding an adult's hand. Fingers only reach an adult's fingertips.
